title: Campomanesia schultziana
intro: Campomanesia schultziana, also known as the Brazilian guava or pitomba, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against infections, reduce inflammation, and neutralize free radicals in the body. The bioactive constituents of Campomanesia schultziana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Campomanesia schultziana is often prepared as a tea or made into a syrup, and is also used in infusions and tinctures to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
